title: Thawing Cryopreserved Human Islets
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"3ptqf","text":"This protocol details the thawing of human islets following their cryopreservation, as performed by the Alberta Diabetes Institute IsletCore. The crypreservation method is detailed in Human Islet Cryopreservation protocol. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":184,"length":28}],"data":{}},{"key":"74que","text":"Human islets cryopreserved and thawed using these methods have been found to retain viability and function after 20 years of cryogenic biobanking.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3c96j","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"dq22e","text":"Manning Fox JE, Lyon J, Dai XQ, Wright RC, Hayward J, van de Bunt M, Kin  T, Shapiro AMJ, McCarthy MI, Gloyn AL, Ungrin MD, Lakey JR, Kneteman  NM, Warnock GL, Korbutt GS, Rajotte RV, MacDonald PE (2015) Human islet function following 20 years of cryogenic biobanking. Diabetologia, 58(7): 1503-1512.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":1,"offset":269,"length":14},{"key":2,"offset":283,"length":16}],"data":{}},{"key":"b006d","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"","data":{"guid":"8F3BA0D02E2811E9BC9FF7EBFCD24069","url":"https://www.protocols.io/view/human-islet-cryopreservation-spcediw"}},"1":{"type":"link","mutability":"","data":{"guid":"B06C43BD938B4214AA5143FFE90A8B5A","url":"http://dx.doi.org/10.1007/s00125-015-3598-4"}},"2":{"type":"link","mutability":"","data":{"guid":"BE01AC184D78400E959EC9607194D152","url":"http://dx.doi.org/10.1007/s00125-015-3598-4"}}}}

steps: A B C D E 1 M199 (10L) 2 Reagent Concentration Mass/Volume Supplier Catalogue # 3 M199 powder 9.41g/L 1 bottle Mediatech-Corning 90050PB 3 4 NaHCO3 26 mM 22.0g Fisher Scientific S233-500 5 HEPES 10 mM 23.83g Fisher Scientific BP310-1 6 Penicillin/Streptomycin 20,000 U/ml penicillin and 20,000 μg/ml streptomycin 50ml Lonza 09-757F Prepare the M199 solution using the above Media Preparation table above: Dispense 9 L of Milli-Q (18mΩ) water into a carboy Store overnight at 4 °C to allow to come to temperature. Using a stirrer add the M199 media powder to the water and allow to mix into solution. Add the powdered supplements and Penicillin/Streptomycin to the appropriate media based on the above table and stir into solution. Stir the solution for 00:30:00 Store the prepared solution overnight at 4 °C to allow all powders to go into the solution Stir the solution for 00:30:00 Calibrate the pH meter using the pH control buffers Adjust the pH level of the M199 solution to pH 7.4 using the NaOH and/or HCl. Bring to the volume of 10L with the appropriate amount of Milli-Q water (18mΩ). Filter into 1L bottles with a 0.22µm nitrocellulose filter.

Heat inactivation of FBS Thaw serum and aliquot into labelled 50ml tubes. If serum was thawed in a refrigerator allow serum to come to room temperature prior to placing in water bath. Fill the water-bath with sufficient water so that the tubes may be submersed to the level of the serum. Set water-bath temperature to maintain the product at 56 °C . Once 56 °C is reached, place the tubes in the water-bath for 00:30:00 . After 30 minutes immediately remove the tubes from the water bath. Store the tubes of heat inactivated FBS at -20 °C .

Prepare 0.75M sucrose in freeze M199 and place on ice. Weigh 12.836 g sucrose and transfer to a 50 ml beaker. Add 30 mL of freeze M199 (as above) and mix until in solution Transfer sucrose solution to a 50 ml volumetric flask, add freeze M199 to bring total volume to 50 ml. Filter sterilize with a 0.22 um cellulose acetate filter.

A B C D E F 1 Reagent CMRL 1066 Bovine serum albumin 30% Insulin-Transferrin-Selenium (100x) Gibco® GlutaMAX Supplement Penicillin-Streptomycin Mixture 2 Volume 500 ml 8.5 ml 5 ml 10 ml 2.5 ml CMRL 1066 - Corning Catalogue number 15110CV Bovine serum albumiin 30% v/v - Equitech Bio Inc. Catalugue number BAL62 Insulin-Transferrin-Selenium (100x) - Corning Catalogue number 25800CR Gibco® GlutaMAX™ Supplement (100x) - ThermoFisher catalogue number 35050061 Penicillin-Streptomycin Mixture - Lomza Catalogue number 09-757F Prepare Human Islet Culture media as described in the table above. Filter sterilize using a Stericup Sterile Vacuum Filtration System.
Remove tubes of cryopreserved human islets from Liquid Nitrogen dewar and place into portable bath containing liquid nitrogen.
Immerse and agitate tubes in 37 °C water bath until almost thawed (WARNING: do not over thaw, remove the tube from the water bath when a small piece of ice is still present).
Centrifuge tubes at 282 x g, 4 °C, 1 minute
Add 1 mL of cold 0.75M sucrose. Lightly hand vortex to get pellet back into suspension. Hold on 0 °C ice slush for 00:30:00 .
Transfer the samples to Room temperature for 00:01:00 .
Continue at Room temperature . Add 1 mL of freeze M199 and lighty mix. Incubate for 00:05:00 .
Add 1 mL of freeze M199 and lighty mix. Incubate for 00:05:00 . Room temperature .
Add 2 mL of freeze M199 and lighty mix. Incubate for 00:05:00 . Room temperature .
Add 4 mL of freeze M199 and lighty mix. Incubate for 00:05:00 Room temperature .
Centrifuge the sample tubes at 282 x g, Room temperature, 1 minute
Remove supernatant and add 5 mL of Human Islet Culture media and lightly hand vortex to allow the islets to go into suspension. Room temperature . .
Using Human Islet Culture media, culture islets at 22 °C 5% CO 2 or use for experiments. It is recommended to hand pick the islets to purity as soon as possible after thawing.

Freeze M199: Prepare Freeze M199 solution as follows: Add 100 mL heat inactivated FBS (HyClone™ Fetal Bovine Serum (Canada), Characterized - Fisher cat# SH3039603) to 900 mL M199. Store at 4 °C . Warm to room temperature for use.
creator: James Lyon
